pattern: The eight-cell update is deterministic with no memory, so the states must eventually cycle; detecting the period collapses a billion days into a tiny remainder.
hints: Each next state depends only on the current one, and there are only finitely many states.
Because the two end cells always become vacant after one step, the reachable states form a short repeating loop.
Find when a state first repeats to learn the cycle length, then advance only the remaining days modulo that length.

edge_inputs.description: huge day count exercises cycle reduction
ends always vacate after day one
exactly one full period of fourteen days

stress.description: random starting state with a large day count to stress the cycle shortcut
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cells = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(8)]
    n = rng.randrange(1, 1000000001)
    return [cells, n]
stress.note: fixed eight cells; n ranges across the full allowed span
